items.requirement: Establish the Data Protection Board of India

implementation.description: Data Protection Board of India established with effect from the date of publication of the notification.

items.requirement: Notify the headquarters of the Board

implementation.description: Head office of the Data Protection Board of India to be in the National Capital Region of India.

items.requirement: Notify the number of Members of the Board besides the Chairperson
Prescribe the manner of appointment of Chairperson and other Members

implementation.description: Rule 17 prescribes Search-cum-Selection Committees for appointment of Chairperson and other Members.

items.requirement: Constitute Search-cum-Selection Committee for appointment of Chairperson
Constitute Search-cum-Selection Committee for appointment of Members other than Chairperson
Prescribe salary, allowances and other terms and conditions of service of Chairperson and Members

implementation.description: Rule 18 and Fifth Schedule prescribe salary (Chairperson: Rs 4,50,000/month; Members: Rs 4,00,000/month) and other terms of service.

items.requirement: Prescribe the procedure for meetings, transaction of business and authentication of orders of the Board

implementation.description: Rule 19 prescribes quorum (one-third), majority voting, casting vote, authentication and inquiry completion timelines.

items.requirement: Prescribe terms and conditions of appointment and service of officers and employees of the Board

implementation.description: Rule 21 and Sixth Schedule prescribe terms of deputation, gratuity, travelling allowance, medical assistance, leave and other conditions.

items.requirement: Prescribe techno-legal measures for the Board to function as a digital office

implementation.description: Rule 20 prescribes that the Board shall function as a digital office and may adopt techno-legal measures for proceedings without physical presence.

items.requirement: Prescribe additional matters in respect of which the Board shall have civil court powers
Prescribe the manner and obligations of a Consent Manager acting on behalf of Data Principals

implementation.description: Rule 4(3) and Part B of First Schedule prescribe 13 obligations of Consent Managers including interoperability, record-keeping, conflict of interest and audit requirements.

items.requirement: Prescribe the manner of registration of Consent Managers and conditions therefor

implementation.description: Rule 4(1)-(2) and Part A of First Schedule prescribe registration process and 9 conditions including minimum net worth of Rs 2 crore, technical capacity, and independent certification.

items.requirement: Prescribe the manner in which a Data Principal may make a complaint to the Board

implementation.description: Rule 3(c)(iii) requires the notice to include a communication link to the Data Fiduciary's website or app for making a complaint to the Board.

items.requirement: Prescribe the form and manner of intimation of personal data breach to the Board and affected Data Principals

implementation.description: Rule 7 prescribes intimation requirements: without delay to Data Principals (nature, consequences, mitigation measures) and to the Board within 72 hours with detailed information.

items.requirement: Prescribe time periods after which specified purpose is deemed no longer served, for different classes of Data Fiduciaries and purposes

implementation.description: Rule 8 and Third Schedule prescribe 3-year retention periods for e-commerce entities (2 crore+ users), online gaming intermediaries (50 lakh+ users), and social media intermediaries (2 crore+ users).

items.requirement: Prescribe the manner of publishing business contact information of the Data Protection Officer or authorised person

implementation.description: Rule 9 requires prominent publication on website or app and mention in every response to Data Principal communications.

items.requirement: Prescribe the period within which the Data Fiduciary or Consent Manager shall respond to grievances

implementation.description: Rule 14(3) prescribes a reasonable period not exceeding 90 days for responding to grievances under the grievance redressal system.

items.requirement: Notify Data Fiduciaries or classes of Data Fiduciaries as Significant Data Fiduciaries
Prescribe additional matters comprising the Data Protection Impact Assessment process

implementation.description: Rule 13(1)-(3) prescribes annual DPIA, audit obligations, reporting to the Board, and due diligence on algorithmic software measures.

items.requirement: Prescribe other measures that a Significant Data Fiduciary shall undertake

implementation.description: Rule 13(3)-(4) prescribes due diligence on algorithmic software and data localization measures for specified personal data.

items.requirement: Specify personal data subject to localization restrictions for Significant Data Fiduciaries, based on committee recommendations
Constitute the committee to recommend personal data subject to localization restrictions
Prescribe the manner of obtaining verifiable consent of parent before processing personal data of a child or person with disability

implementation.description: Rules 10 and 11 prescribe verifiable consent mechanisms: identity/age verification via reliable data held by Data Fiduciary, voluntarily provided details, or virtual tokens from authorised entities.

items.requirement: Prescribe classes of Data Fiduciaries and purposes exempted from verifiable consent and tracking restrictions for children

implementation.description: Rule 12 and Fourth Schedule prescribe exemptions: Part A lists 5 classes (healthcare, educational institutions, child care, transport) and Part B lists 6 purposes (statutory functions, government benefits, email, location tracking, content filtering, age verification).

items.requirement: Notify Data Fiduciaries whose processing of children's data is verifiably safe, specifying the age above which consent obligations are relaxed
Notify Digital Locker service providers for the purpose of verifiable consent for children's data processing
Notify countries or territories to which transfer of personal data by a Data Fiduciary is restricted
Specify requirements for making personal data available to foreign States, persons or entities under control of a foreign State
Specify personal data of Significant Data Fiduciaries subject to localization (no transfer outside India)
Prescribe the subsidies, benefits, services, certificates, licences or permits for which the State may process personal data as a legitimate use

implementation.description: Rule 5 and Second Schedule prescribe standards for processing by State instrumentalities under Section 7(b), including lawfulness, purpose limitation, data minimization, accuracy and security safeguards.

items.requirement: Notify databases, registers, books or other documents maintained by the State for the purpose of legitimate use processing
Prescribe standards for processing personal data for research, archiving or statistical purposes

implementation.description: Rule 16 and Second Schedule prescribe standards for research processing: lawfulness, purpose limitation, data minimization, accuracy, retention limits, security safeguards, intimation and accountability.

items.requirement: Notify Data Fiduciaries or classes including startups exempted from notice, data accuracy/erasure and SDF obligations
Prescribe the manner in which a Data Principal may make a request to access information about her personal data

implementation.description: Rule 14(1)-(2) requires Data Fiduciaries to publish means for exercising rights and accept requests using those means with identifying particulars.

items.requirement: Prescribe any other information related to personal data and its processing that a Data Principal may access

implementation.description: Rule 14(1)(a)-(b) prescribes details of means for exercising rights and identifier particulars required for identification under terms of service.

items.requirement: Prescribe the manner in which a Data Principal may make a request for erasure of her personal data

implementation.description: Rule 14(1)-(2) prescribes a unified mechanism for exercising rights including erasure, via means published by the Data Fiduciary on its website or app.

items.requirement: Prescribe the manner in which a Data Principal may nominate another individual to exercise her rights in the event of death or incapacity

implementation.description: Rule 14(4) prescribes nomination via means and particulars required by the Data Fiduciary, in accordance with its terms of service and applicable law.

items.requirement: Prescribe the form, manner and fee for filing an appeal to the Appellate Tribunal

implementation.description: Rule 22(1)-(2) prescribes digital filing of appeals with fee equivalent to TRAI Act appeal fees, payable via UPI or authorised payment systems, with discretionary reduction or waiver.

items.requirement: Prescribe the procedure for dealing with appeals before the Appellate Tribunal

implementation.description: Rule 22(3) prescribes that the Appellate Tribunal shall be guided by principles of natural justice, may regulate its own procedure, and shall function as a digital office with techno-legal measures.
